agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Evolution of the Shipment aggregate from a 1:1 Shipment-Recipient relationship to a 1:N relationship. This is a fundamental domain model change affecting the core bounded context of shipment management, with cascading impacts on data migration, partner integrations, and downstream business processes like proof-of-delivery, notifications, and billing.","bounded_context":"Shipment Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Shipment","definition":"A logistical unit representing goods in transit from origin to one or more destinations. The aggregate root that owns Recipients."},{"term":"Recipient","definition":"A party designated to receive goods from a Shipment. Identified by name, address, phone number, and now also delivery preferences. A Recipient belongs to exactly one Shipment."},{"term":"Delivery Preferences","definition":"Recipient-level configuration governing how and when delivery should occur (e.g., time windows, safe-place authorization, signature requirements, alternate drop-off instructions)."},{"term":"Primary Recipient","definition":"The first or default Recipient on a multi-recipient Shipment. Critical for backward compatibility — legacy integrations and single-recipient workflows must resolve to this entity."},{"term":"Proof of Delivery (POD)","definition":"Confirmation that goods were delivered to a specific Recipient. In a multi-recipient model, POD must be captured per-Recipient, not per-Shipment."},{"term":"Partner API","definition":"External integrations that consume shipment data in the current single-recipient format. Must be migrated or adapted via a compatibility layer."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A Shipment must have at least one Recipient at all times.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A Shipment without a Recipient has no delivery destination and is logistically meaningless — it corrupts the state of the system.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT shipment.recipients.count >= 1 at creation and after any modification"},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Each Recipient within a Shipment must have a unique delivery address.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Two Recipients at the same address on the same Shipment is a data integrity issue — it implies a duplicate, not a legitimate multi-drop. If the same address needs two deliveries, those are two Shipments.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT no two recipients on the same shipment share an identical address"},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Every Recipient must have a name, address, and phone number — delivery preferences are optional with system defaults.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Name, address, and phone are the minimum required to attempt delivery and contact the recipient. Without these, the courier cannot fulfill the shipment.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT recipient.name IS NOT NULL AND recipient.address IS NOT NULL AND recipient.phone IS NOT NULL"},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Shipment status transitions must account for per-Recipient delivery outcomes. A Shipment is 'Delivered' only when ALL Recipients have received their goods.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Marking a Shipment as delivered when only some Recipients have received goods misrepresents fulfillment state and breaks downstream billing and SLA calculations.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT shipment.status = 'Delivered' ONLY WHEN all recipients have status = 'Delivered'"},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Existing single-recipient shipments must be migrated to the new model by wrapping the existing Recipient in a collection of size 1, preserving all original data.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a migration policy, not a state integrity constraint. The approach could change (e.g., lazy migration vs. bulk migration).","testable_assertion":"ASSERT post-migration: every legacy shipment has exactly one Recipient in the new collection format with identical name, address, and phone"},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Partner APIs that consume shipment data must continue to receive a single-recipient payload until they explicitly opt into the multi-recipient format.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a compatibility policy that can be changed as partners migrate. A VP could set a sunset date.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT for non-opted-in partners: API response contains a single recipient object (the Primary Recipient), not an array"},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"Each Recipient on a multi-recipient Shipment must be independently notifiable — notifications (SMS, email) are sent per-Recipient, not per-Shipment.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Sending a single notification for a multi-recipient shipment would leave some recipients uninformed, violating the core communication contract of a delivery platform.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT notification_count >= shipment.recipients.count for each notification event type"},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"Delivery preferences apply at the Recipient level, not the Shipment level. If no preferences are set, system defaults apply.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"The entire purpose of the model change is per-Recipient preferences. Applying preferences at the Shipment level defeats the business intent and corrupts delivery instructions.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT delivery_preferences are stored and evaluated per Recipient, not inherited from Shipment"}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A new Shipment is being created","when":"the creator specifies multiple Recipients with distinct addresses and delivery preferences","then":"the Shipment is created with all Recipients persisted, each with their own preferences, and the first Recipient is designated as Primary Recipient"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"An existing legacy Shipment with one Recipient in the old schema","when":"the data migration runs","then":"the Shipment now has a recipients collection of size 1 containing the original Recipient data with no data loss, and that Recipient is marked as Primary"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A partner API integration that has NOT opted into multi-recipient format","when":"it requests shipment data for a multi-recipient Shipment","then":"it receives a response with only the Primary Recipient in the legacy single-recipient payload format"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A multi-recipient Shipment where 2 of 3 Recipients have been delivered","when":"the system evaluates Shipment status","then":"the Shipment status remains 'In Progress' (or 'Partially Delivered'), not 'Delivered'"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A multi-recipient Shipment","when":"a delivery event occurs for one Recipient","then":"only that Recipient's status is updated, and a notification is sent to that specific Recipient only"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A user attempts to create a Shipment with zero Recipients","when":"the creation request is submitted","then":"the system rejects the request with a validation error"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A user attempts to add a Recipient with the same address as an existing Recipient on the Shipment","when":"the add-recipient request is submitted","then":"the system rejects the request with a duplicate address error"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A multi-recipient Shipment","when":"all Recipients have status 'Delivered'","then":"the Shipment status transitions to 'Delivered' and billing/SLA calculations are finalized"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Recipient is removed from a multi-recipient Shipment after goods are already in transit","expected_behavior":"System must define whether removal is allowed mid-transit. Recommendation: disallow removal once Shipment is in 'In Transit' or later status. Goods are physically loaded — you cannot un-deliver mid-route."},{"scenario":"Two Recipients on the same Shipment have conflicting delivery preferences (e.g., one requires signature, one says leave at door) and the courier is visiting both on the same route","expected_behavior":"Each Recipient's preferences are honored independently. The courier app must present per-stop instructions. No preference merging or averaging."},{"scenario":"Partner API receives a webhook for a multi-recipient Shipment but is on legacy format","expected_behavior":"Webhook payload must be filtered to Primary Recipient only. If the event pertains to a non-Primary Recipient, the partner either receives no webhook or receives it attributed to the Primary Recipient (policy decision needed)."},{"scenario":"A Shipment has 50+ Recipients (bulk distribution scenario)","expected_behavior":"System must enforce a maximum Recipient count per Shipment to prevent abuse and operational complexity. The specific limit is a policy decision but must exist."},{"scenario":"Primary Recipient is deleted or changed on a multi-recipient Shipment","expected_behavior":"System must always maintain exactly one Primary Recipient. If the current Primary is removed, the next Recipient in order is promoted automatically. A Shipment can never have zero or multiple Primary Recipients."},{"scenario":"Legacy report or analytics query assumes 1:1 Shipment-Recipient","expected_behavior":"Queries must be updated or a compatibility view must exist that flattens multi-recipient Shipments into one row per Recipient (fan-out) to avoid undercounting."},{"scenario":"Partial delivery failure — one Recipient's address is invalid but others are valid","expected_behavior":"The Shipment continues for valid Recipients. The failed Recipient enters a resolution workflow (address correction, return). The Shipment is not failed wholesale."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Multi-recipient shipments can increase revenue per shipment by consolidating multiple deliveries. However, migration costs (engineering, testing, partner coordination) and potential billing model changes (per-recipient vs per-shipment pricing) require careful planning. Incorrect billing during transition could cause revenue leakage.","operational":"Significant operational complexity increase: courier route optimization must handle multiple stops per Shipment, proof-of-delivery tracking multiplies, partial delivery states must be managed, and customer support must understand the new model. Dispatch systems, warehouse pick/pack, and last-mile delivery apps all need updates.","user":"Senders gain flexibility to ship to multiple recipients in one transaction. Recipients get personalized delivery preferences. However, tracking UX must be rethought — a single tracking number now maps to multiple delivery outcomes, which can confuse end users if not designed carefully.","risk":"HIGH. This is a core domain model change. Risks include: (1) data migration corruption of 2M existing shipments, (2) partner API breakage if compatibility layer has gaps, (3) incorrect shipment status calculations during transition, (4) billing errors on multi-recipient shipments. Recommend phased rollout with feature flag.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Senders/Shippers","impact":"New capability to create multi-recipient shipments. Must update their integration if using API. UI/UX changes in shipment creation flow."},{"group":"Recipients","impact":"Delivery preferences per recipient is a positive UX improvement. Tracking experience changes — must clearly communicate which part of the shipment is theirs."},{"group":"Couriers/Drivers","impact":"Route and delivery workflow changes. Must handle per-recipient instructions. Proof-of-delivery collected per recipient. App updates required."},{"group":"External Partners (API consumers)","impact":"Breaking change if not managed. Compatibility layer is mandatory. Partners need migration timeline and opt-in mechanism for new format."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Must be trained on new model. Partial delivery states create new support scenarios. Tooling must show per-recipient status."},{"group":"Finance/Billing","impact":"Pricing model may need revision. Per-shipment vs per-recipient billing must be decided. Revenue recognition for partially delivered shipments is a new scenario."},{"group":"Engineering/Data","impact":"Schema migration of 2M records. Analytics pipelines, reports, and dashboards must be updated. API versioning strategy required."}]},"confidence":{"level":52,"reasoning":"The product team's request is clear in intent (1:N recipients with preferences) but leaves several critical business policy decisions undefined: delivery preference schema, maximum recipients per shipment, billing model changes, partner migration timeline, partial delivery status naming, and Primary Recipient semantics. These are not contradictions but genuine ambiguities in the spec that must be resolved before implementation.","high_confidence_areas":["Core invariant: Shipment must have >= 1 Recipient","Per-Recipient delivery preferences is the correct modeling approach","Partner API backward compatibility is mandatory","Data migration must be lossless for existing 2M shipments","Shipment status must reflect aggregate of all Recipient delivery outcomes"],"low_confidence_areas":["What specific delivery preferences are supported (time windows, signature, safe-place, etc.) — not defined","Maximum number of Recipients per Shipment — not specified","Billing model: per-shipment or per-recipient pricing — not decided","Primary Recipient concept: is it explicit or implicit (first in list)? — not specified","Partial delivery status naming and state machine — not defined","Partner webhook behavior for non-Primary Recipient events — not decided","Whether Recipients can be modified after Shipment creation and at which statuses — not specified"],"assumptions":["Primary Recipient concept will exist for backward compatibility","Delivery preferences have sensible system defaults when not explicitly set","Partner API compatibility layer will use API versioning, not a separate endpoint","Data migration will be performed as a bulk operation, not lazy-on-read"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The spec says 'each Recipient can have different delivery preferences' but does not define what delivery preferences consist of. Is it time windows? Signature requirements? Safe-place authorization? Contactless delivery? The preference schema fundamentally affects the domain model, validation rules, and courier app UX.","affected_stakeholders":["Product Team","Engineering","Couriers","Recipients"],"options":["Define a fixed enum of preference types (time_window, signature_required, safe_place, special_instructions)","Allow freeform key-value preferences with no schema enforcement","Start with a minimal set (signature + special_instructions) and expand iteratively"],"recommendation":"Start with a minimal, well-defined preference schema (signature_required: bool, delivery_instructions: string, time_window: enum) and expand based on user feedback. Freeform preferences create downstream chaos for couriers."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The spec does not define how Shipment-level status works when Recipients have different delivery outcomes. What are the intermediate states? Is there a 'Partially Delivered' status? What status transitions are valid?","affected_stakeholders":["Engineering","Customer Support","Senders","Recipients"],"options":["Add a 'Partially Delivered' status to the Shipment state machine","Keep existing statuses and only transition to 'Delivered' when all Recipients are delivered","Introduce per-Recipient status as the primary view, with Shipment status being derived"],"recommendation":"Introduce per-Recipient status tracking with Shipment status derived as the worst-case aggregate. Add 'Partially Delivered' as an explicit Shipment status to avoid ambiguity in reporting and SLA calculations."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Billing model for multi-recipient shipments is not addressed. Is a 3-recipient shipment billed as 1 shipment or 3? This affects pricing, invoicing, and revenue recognition. It also affects whether 'Shipment' remains the billing unit or if 'Delivery' (per-Recipient) becomes the new billing unit.","affected_stakeholders":["Finance/Billing","Senders","Product Team"],"options":["Bill per Shipment regardless of recipient count (simpler, may undercharge)","Bill per Recipient/delivery stop (more accurate, more complex)","Base rate per Shipment + incremental fee per additional Recipient (hybrid)"],"recommendation":"Hybrid model: base shipment fee + per-additional-recipient surcharge. This preserves backward compatibility for single-recipient billing while fairly pricing the additional operational cost of multi-drop deliveries."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Partner API backward compatibility strategy is not specified beyond the implicit requirement. Need to define: API versioning approach, partner opt-in mechanism, sunset timeline for legacy format, and webhook behavior for multi-recipient events sent to legacy partners.","affected_stakeholders":["External Partners","Engineering","Product Team"],"options":["API versioning (v1 = legacy single-recipient, v2 = multi-recipient)","Feature flag per partner with gradual migration","Dual-write: always send both formats during transition period"],"recommendation":"API versioning with v1 frozen as legacy compatibility layer. Partners migrate to v2 on their timeline with a published sunset date (recommend 12-18 months). Webhooks for legacy partners include only Primary Recipient events."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}

content.thinking: Let me analyze this domain model evolution from single-recipient to multi-recipient shipments.

Partners migrate to v2 on their timeline with a published sunset date (recommend 12-18 months). Webhooks for legacy partners include only Primary Recipient events."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}

trace.result: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Evolution of the Shipment aggregate from a 1:1 Shipment-Recipient relationship to a 1:N relationship. This is a fundamental domain model change affecting the core bounded context of shipment management, with cascading impacts on data migration, partner integrations, and downstream business processes like proof-of-delivery, notifications, and billing.","bounded_context":"Shipment Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Shipment","definition":"A logistical unit representing goods in transit from origin to one or more destinations. The aggregate root that owns Recipients."},{"term":"Recipient","definition":"A party designated to receive goods from a Shipment. Identified by name, address, phone number, and now also delivery preferences. A Recipient belongs to exactly one Shipment."},{"term":"Delivery Preferences","definition":"Recipient-level configuration governing how and when delivery should occur (e.g., time windows, safe-place authorization, signature requirements, alternate drop-off instructions)."},{"term":"Primary Recipient","definition":"The first or default Recipient on a multi-recipient Shipment. Critical for backward compatibility — legacy integrations and single-recipient workflows must resolve to this entity."},{"term":"Proof of Delivery (POD)","definition":"Confirmation that goods were delivered to a specific Recipient. In a multi-recipient model, POD must be captured per-Recipient, not per-Shipment."},{"term":"Partner API","definition":"External integrations that consume shipment data in the current single-recipient format. Must be migrated or adapted via a compatibility layer."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A Shipment must have at least one Recipient at all times.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A Shipment without a Recipient has no delivery destination and is logistically meaningless — it corrupts the state of the system.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT shipment.recipients.count >= 1 at creation and after any modification"},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Each Recipient within a Shipment must have a unique delivery address.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Two Recipients at the same address on the same Shipment is a data integrity issue — it implies a duplicate, not a legitimate multi-drop. If the same address needs two deliveries, those are two Shipments.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT no two recipients on the same shipment share an identical address"},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Every Recipient must have a name, address, and phone number — delivery preferences are optional with system defaults.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Name, address, and phone are the minimum required to attempt delivery and contact the recipient. Without these, the courier cannot fulfill the shipment.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT recipient.name IS NOT NULL AND recipient.address IS NOT NULL AND recipient.phone IS NOT NULL"},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Shipment status transitions must account for per-Recipient delivery outcomes. A Shipment is 'Delivered' only when ALL Recipients have received their goods.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Marking a Shipment as delivered when only some Recipients have received goods misrepresents fulfillment state and breaks downstream billing and SLA calculations.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT shipment.status = 'Delivered' ONLY WHEN all recipients have status = 'Delivered'"},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Existing single-recipient shipments must be migrated to the new model by wrapping the existing Recipient in a collection of size 1, preserving all original data.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a migration policy, not a state integrity constraint. existing legacy Shipment with one Recipient in the old schema","when":"the data migration runs","then":"the Shipment now has a recipients collection of size 1 containing the original Recipient data
